items.briefing:
A new OAIC survey confirms Australians are increasingly concerned about their privacy and have low trust in how AI systems use their personal data. This is particularly relevant for RTOs, which collect significant amounts of student and staff information — ensure your data handling practices are transparent and compliant with Australian privacy law.

A reminder that cyber risk in education extends beyond campus systems to include remote learning tools, third-party platforms, and personal devices. Practical tip: Review what student data your organisation shares with third-party apps and remove access for any tools no longer in active use.

featured.title: Australians more concerned about privacy as trust in AI remains low, OAIC survey finds
featured.quote: Australians' plummeting AI trust and privacy concerns directly impact regulatory and business practices nationwide.
